title: The Week That Was, The Week Ahead: Macro and Markets, June 28
summary: TipRanks reported the June 28 weekly snapshot: DJIA -0.09% to 51,876.11, SPX -0.05% to 7,354.02, Nasdaq/NDX -1.09% to 29,118.24, 10Y Treasury yield 4.38%, oil -3.75% to $70.07, gold +1.56% to $4,083.50, and Bitcoin +0.28% to $59,923.97. It framed the week as risk reduction and rotation into safer sectors amid AI-spending, rates, valuation and regulation concerns.
extracted_text: Published Jun 28, 2026, 04:26 AM. Stocks ended the week lower as investors pulled back from risk assets and rotated into safer parts of the market. TipRanks listed DJIA -0.09% at 51,876.11, SPX -0.05% at 7,354.02, Nasdaq/NDX -1.09% at 29,118.24, 10-year U.S. Treasury yield 4.38%, oil -3.75% at $70.07, gold +1.56% at $4,083.50 and Bitcoin +0.28% at $59,923.97. The source identified AI spending, rates, valuation and regulation as the pressure points.

title: How major US stock indexes fared Friday 6/26/2026
summary: AP/Barchart reported Friday closes: S&P 500 -3.47 points to 7,354.02, Dow -44.51 to 51,876.11, Nasdaq -60.99 to 25,297.62, Russell 2000 +2.23 to 3,010.08. Weekly moves: S&P 500 -146.56/-2.0%, Dow +311.41/+0.6%, Nasdaq -1,220.31/-4.6%, Russell 2000 +30.32/+1.0%. YTD: S&P +7.4%, Dow +7.9%, Nasdaq +8.8%, Russell +21.3%.
extracted_text: Published Fri Jun 26, 2026, 3:25 PM CDT. AP/Barchart: S&P 500 slipped less than 0.1% Friday to 7,354.02; Dow fell 0.1% to 51,876.11; Nasdaq lost 0.2% to 25,297.62; Russell 2000 rose 0.1% to 3,010.08. Weekly point/% changes were S&P 500 -146.56/-2.0%, Dow +311.41/+0.6%, Nasdaq -1,220.31/-4.6%, Russell 2000 +30.32/+1.0%.

title: Debt, AI boom and economic fragilities raise global risks, BIS says
summary: CNBC reported the BIS Annual Economic Report warned on high public debt, inflation pressures, financial fragilities and AI-boom uncertainty. BIS General Manager Pablo Hernandez de Cos said policy actions must reinforce each other and that policymakers must act now; Frank Smets warned the new sovereign-financial stability nexus may mean more frequent and sharper drops in sovereign bond values.
extracted_text: CNBC June 28, 2026 reported the Bank for International Settlements warned that high public debt, inflation pressures, financial fragilities and uncertainty around the AI boom are raising risks. Pablo Hernandez de Cos said policy actions must reinforce each other and policymakers must act now. Frank Smets said the new fiscal-financial stability nexus may mean more frequent and sharper drops in sovereign bond values.

title: Trump again threatens Iran with annihilation as Kuwait and Bahrain report attacks
summary: CNBC reported U.S. strikes on 10 Iranian military targets near the Strait of Hormuz after a drone strike on the Panamanian-flagged tanker M/T Kiku carrying more than 2M barrels of crude. Kuwait and Bahrain reported missile/drone attacks. CNBC also reported Friday oil settlements: Brent August -4.34% to $71.99/bbl; WTI August -3.74% to $69.23/bbl, as more tankers exited Hormuz.
extracted_text: CNBC June 28, 2026: U.S. Central Command said U.S. fighter jets struck 10 Iranian military targets in and near the Strait of Hormuz early Sunday after a drone strike on Panamanian-flagged tanker M/T Kiku carrying more than two million barrels of crude. Kuwait and Bahrain reported missile and drone attacks. CNBC cited Brent August down 4.34% to $71.99/barrel and WTI August down 3.74% to $69.23/barrel Friday.
